Package de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.object_model

Interface ISymbolicValue

All Superinterfaces:
ISymbolicElement
All Known Implementing Classes:
SymbolicLayoutReader.KeYlessValue, SymbolicValue
public interface ISymbolicValue extends ISymbolicElement

Represents a variable of an ISymbolicState or ISymbolicObject which contains a value of a primitive type.

The default implementation is SymbolicValue.

See Also:
  • SymbolicValue

    Description

    Returns the represented array index or null if a program variable is represented..

    • isArrayIndex

      boolean isArrayIndex()
      Checks if an array index or a program variable is represented.
      Returns:
      true array index, false program variable.

      Returns the represented IProgramVariable or null if an array index is represented.

    • getProgramVariableString

      String getProgramVariableString()
      Returns the represented IProgramVariable as human readable String or null if an array index is represented.

      The represented IProgramVariable as human readable String or null if an array index is represented.

    • getValue

      Term getValue()
      Returns the value of the represented variable.
      Returns:

    • getValueString

      String getValueString()
      Returns the value of the represented variable as human readable String.

      The value of the represented variable as human readable String.

    • getType

      Sort getType()
      Returns the type of the value.
      Returns:
      The type of the value.

    • getTypeString

      String getTypeString()
      Returns the type of the value as human readable string.
      Returns:
      The type of the value as human readable string.

    • getCondition

      Term getCondition()

      Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid.

      The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

      Returns:
      The optional condition under which this value is valid.

    • getConditionString

      String getConditionString()

      Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid as human readable String.

      The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

      Returns:
      The optional condition under which this value is valid as human readable String.
